paragraphs: When Silence births the song — when synapses fire — get ready.[#pause] Place a cup to your ear and listen.[#pause2] First, the rain. Drip. Drip. Drip.[#pause] That's me.[#pause2] Then the ocean. Shhhhh — whooosh. The long breath of water rolling over itself, foam whispering against the shore.[#pause2] Now you. Thum… thum… thum… behind the ribs.

sections.heading:
30 CLICKS POST UNFOLDING 
CAPTAIN'S READY ROOM, FNS DÁINN
paragraphs: [#high-pitched-whine]Screeeeee.
A shriek spiked through the speakers. A needle-thin feedback pinching through the eardrum directly to the jaw.
Stacey yelped. The captain flinched.

paragraphs.text: "[wincing]Did you hear that?" Stacey asked.
paragraphs.speaker: stacey-kiran

paragraphs.text: "[concerned]Yes. Feedback? How?"
paragraphs.speaker: benjamin-westhoff

paragraphs.text: "[over comms][focused]Strange. I don't know. Say again."
paragraphs.speaker: stacey-kiran

paragraphs.text: "[urgent]Stacey. You haven't left. This is your last chance. Any longer and I can't retrieve you. Go now."
paragraphs.speaker: benjamin-westhoff

paragraphs: The *Dáinn* was a click from orbital entry around Mairee, and that meant the end of Westhoff's command. Kiran and Hale had not yet returned.
Stacey blinked.

paragraphs.text: "[over comms][steady]Hale and I discussed it. And we ran our own tactical analysis."
paragraphs.speaker: stacey-kiran

paragraphs.text: "[low]And?" Ben asked, knowing the answer.
paragraphs.speaker: benjamin-westhoff

paragraphs.text: "[over comms][reporting]The Stingray is a unique asset," she stated, as if reading from a report. "[reporting]If we return to the *Dáinn* or stay here, the Directorate will impound her. We agree its strategic value is highest operating as an independent."
paragraphs.speaker: stacey-kiran

paragraphs: Ben heard Hale's plea in Kiran's words.

paragraphs.text: "[gentle]Is that *your* choice?"
paragraphs.speaker: benjamin-westhoff

paragraphs.text: "[over comms][admitting]Well. Hale's final decision," she admitted, a faint, reluctant smile touching her lips. "[wry]My analysis resonates with his emotional plea. Which is, for once, strategically sound."
paragraphs.speaker: stacey-kiran

paragraphs.text: Ben managed a smile. "[fond]But you? What do you want?"
paragraphs.speaker: benjamin-westhoff

paragraphs.text: "[over comms][quiet]I'm where I need to be, Captain. With Alis."
paragraphs.speaker: stacey-kiran

paragraphs.text: He nodded, swallowing his concern. "[steady]Understood, Stingray. Patch me to Gupta. And…" Ben took a shaky breath. "[soft]Godspeed."
paragraphs.speaker: benjamin-westhoff

paragraphs: When Ben ended the call, [#chime]the console screen flickered, signaling a new connection. Gupta's life-sized image appeared before Ben, now in the ship's Observation Dome. Behind him, the huge viewport was covered in swirling algae.

paragraphs.text: "[resonant]Bennn," Gupta hummed, resonating in the room like a gong.
paragraphs.speaker: rajendra-gupta

paragraphs.text: Ben frowned. "[direct]One click, Raj. I'm handing the *Dáinn* over. Everything's in place. What will you do when you arrive?"
paragraphs.speaker: benjamin-westhoff

paragraphs.text: "[serene]Our intention is unchanged," Gupta replied. His face was a mask of terrifying serenity. "[serene]We will reach the Wellspring before it runs dry. We will rejoin it."
paragraphs.speaker: rajendra-gupta

paragraphs.text: "[pressing]And what if Corbin has other plans for the *Bris*?" Ben pressed.
paragraphs.speaker: benjamin-westhoff

paragraphs.text: Gupta laughed, a soft, gentle sound. "[amused]He cannot hold us. He's rusted iron. We seep through."
paragraphs.speaker: rajendra-gupta

paragraphs.text: "[seizing]We?" Ben seized on the word. "[urgent]What am I to call your collective?"
paragraphs.speaker: benjamin-westhoff

paragraphs: He felt a desperate need to label the nightmare.

paragraphs.text: Gupta's expression turned serious. "[grave]You might call us Seiðr," he said.
paragraphs.speaker: rajendra-gupta

paragraphs: The word was a sibilant whisper, a wet, rustling sound like wind through a kelp forest. To Ben's ear, it was a blurred symphony of meanings — *Seeder, See-er, Seeper.* It sounded like an invitation and a threat, layered on top of each other.

paragraphs.text: "[pleading]Raj, this is our last call for now. I trust we'll meet again. But promise me that the crew, our people…[#pause] You'll treat them as well as I would."
paragraphs.speaker: benjamin-westhoff

paragraphs: Ben felt a sudden, sharp pang of grief. The Raj he had served with — the man who liked bad scotch and talked extensively about his daughter's engineering exams — was but a ghost of his past self, wearing a golden mask. He felt the crushing weight of his own helplessness.

paragraphs.text: Gupta gave a deliberate nod. "[whispered]The song will not force anyone to sing, as agreed," he whispered. "[serene]Though they are a part of us now. Their faith is not yours, nor mine. They will be free to interact with the whole as they will. Take whatever form. Until —"
paragraphs.speaker: rajendra-gupta

paragraphs: His gaze went past Ben then, as if seeing the future shape of their journey.

paragraphs.text: "[soft]Goodbye for now, Ben. I will see you…[#pause] below."
paragraphs.speaker: rajendra-gupta

paragraphs: Ben stood quietly for a while. He thought about the crew he was leaving behind and the bleak future that waited for him on the Plateau. He opened a cabinet drawer and took out a plaque and a framed holo-photo. In the photo, a young woman with his stubborn eyes and a fearless smile stood next to him, her arm around his. Ben sat down and placed the frame on the table. He rubbed his tired face as if to shake the memory.

She was gone, somewhere beyond the Fold. At least she was far away from this forsaken hole in the universe. She was with her mother, his ex-wife. That thought was a dark comfort. He remembered her laugh and their last hug at the transit gate. He had promised they would meet again. A warm tear ran down his cheek.
[#tap]He pressed his comm.

paragraphs.text: "[steady]Jia. My ready room."
paragraphs.speaker: benjamin-westhoff

paragraphs: [#doorhiss]She arrived soon after, looking expectant.

paragraphs.text: "[grave]Our path is grim," Ben began, his voice regaining command. "[grave]I will hand over this ship. I'm relieving myself of its command, but not of my duty. This ship is yours now, Ionie. I am promoting you to captain."
paragraphs.speaker: benjamin-westhoff

paragraphs: She lost her steady composure, eyes wide.
[#soft-thud-land]He flipped the heavy plaque, showing the engraved ship's motto: *PORTAMUS FUTURUM.*

paragraphs.text: "[quiet]We carry the future. I had this made before we left Sol," he said quietly. "[soft]I wanted to give it to you after we arrived, in a better ceremony. But now is the time."
paragraphs.speaker: benjamin-westhoff

paragraphs: He handed it to her.

paragraphs.text: "[solemn]Even though it speaks of the future, it also holds our past, our memories, and everything we've lost. This burden is an honor, Captain."
paragraphs.speaker: benjamin-westhoff

paragraphs: Jia's eyes welled up.

paragraphs.text: "[shaken]Sir, Corbin…[#pause] He'll just…[#pause] he'll replace me."
paragraphs.speaker: ionie-jia

paragraphs.text: "[wry]Replacing me — that's politics. But the ship… she's another matter," Ben said, a flicker of his old weary smile returning. "[warm]She needs someone who truly knows her. No one knows her better than you, Ionie. That kind of knowledge isn't replaceable."
paragraphs.speaker: benjamin-westhoff

paragraphs: He winked. She quickly brushed at her eyes with the back of her hand.

paragraphs.text: "[steady]Yes, Captain."
paragraphs.speaker: ionie-jia

paragraphs: She smiled.

paragraphs.text: "[approving]Good."
paragraphs.speaker: benjamin-westhoff

paragraphs: Ben picked up a slate with a wooden cover.

paragraphs.text: "[low]This is the captain's log. It has a complete, unedited report on everything: the *Bris*, Dr. Martel's analysis, and the Emergency Suppression Protocol."
paragraphs.speaker: benjamin-westhoff

paragraphs: He paused, as if unsure about how to continue.

paragraphs.text: "[careful]You will learn more about the nature of our mission. And it might upset you, but remember: we're leading humanity to its future. There's more to tell, more than I can say now. It's all written here."
paragraphs.speaker: benjamin-westhoff

paragraphs.text: "[reverent]Understood," she said, and she took the slate from him as if it were a sacred text.
paragraphs.speaker: ionie-jia

paragraphs: He stood up from his chair and looked at her one more time.

paragraphs.text: "[steady]I will inform the crew in a ship-wide address in four beats," he said as another lonely tear ran down his cheek. "[soft]This is your ship now."
paragraphs.speaker: benjamin-westhoff

paragraphs: He walked toward the door, but before exiting, he put a hand on her shoulder.

paragraphs.text: "[gentle]Just be you. You'll do fine."
paragraphs.speaker: benjamin-westhoff

paragraphs: And with the photo frame tucked under his arm, [#doorhiss]he left the captain's ready room.

sections.heading: QUARTERMASTER'S OFFICE, FNS DÁINN
paragraphs: Humanity, Steven Gail believed, was a poorly written play, full of overwrought characters who took their parts far too seriously. He believed he owed his genius to the knowledge that he was sitting in the audience.

One wall held a real wooden bookshelf, packed with books by long-dead philosophers and poets. Next to a chessboard with a game left unfinished sat a crystal decanter. Steven stood in front of a full-length mirror, fixing his jacket cuffs. His reflection looked bored.
[#chime]His door chimed.

paragraphs.text: "[bored]Enter," Steven said to his reflection.
paragraphs.speaker: steven-gail

paragraphs: Dominic Beltrán slumped in.

paragraphs.text: "[breezy]Found the kid yet, Stevie?"
paragraphs.speaker: dominic-beltrán

paragraphs: Steven gestured to a schematic of the Compassionate Oversight Grid.

paragraphs.text: "[sardonic]Exquisite, really. A system built on the naive assumption that comfort doesn't have a cost. We spent a century containing our most dangerous creations, and the moment we succeed, we grow complacent. Humans adore the feeling of being kept docile. Honestly, give us a pacifier and put us in a crib already."
paragraphs.speaker: steven-gail

paragraphs.text: Dom considered his words. "[probing]Can you break Corbin's Grid?"
paragraphs.speaker: dominic-beltrán

paragraphs.text: "[amused]Break it?" Steven chuckled, clearly amused.
paragraphs.speaker: steven-gail

paragraphs: He poured two glasses of whiskey and gave one to Beltrán.

paragraphs.text: "[explaining]The king thinks the board is his. But Kaspar Savannen, bless his earnest heart, created the game. We can't break it, but now that we know the rules, we can change them. So long as…" He nodded at Oliver's digital profile. "[focused]The boy is the key."
paragraphs.speaker: steven-gail

paragraphs.text: "[curious]So you've reached him?" Dom asked.
paragraphs.speaker: dominic-beltrán

paragraphs.text: "[satisfied]Now that we're close enough, Jia has agreed to tight-beam our whispers through Orbital Three into the Homestead's civilian network. Hard times seem to bring back an appreciation for poetry," Steven said with a wry smile.
paragraphs.speaker: steven-gail

paragraphs: [#tapping]His fingers moved over his console like a pianist.

paragraphs.text: "[showing]Here. This is the clue Muro left for us."
paragraphs.speaker: steven-gail

paragraphs: He pulled up Muro's social feed on the open network. On the screen, the first verse glowed softly.

THE WEAVER LOST HIS WEB, BENEATH A FALLING SKY. 
THE NET IS TORN, THE THREADS ARE BARE, 
WHERE NOW DOES THE EGG LIE?

paragraphs.text: "[satisfied]And here," Steven said to Dom, a note of satisfaction in his voice as a new reply appeared beneath it, "[satisfied]is our kid's answer. He's picked up the thread."
paragraphs.speaker: steven-gail

paragraphs:
THE MAP IS IN MY HEAD, THE EGG STILL IN ITS NEST. 
UNTIL THE WATCHER FEEDS OR SLEEPS, 
I AM TRAPPED BUT TRY MY BEST.
A chuckle escaped Steven's lips. He eased into his chair and swirled whiskey, watching it catch the light.

paragraphs.text: "[judging]Crude, but clear," he judged. "[reading]He knows where it is but cannot reach it yet. It's guarded."
paragraphs.speaker: steven-gail

paragraphs.text: Dominic pointed to Steven's console. "[directing]Pull up the diagnostic for the grav-cyclers. The maintenance calibration log."
paragraphs.speaker: dominic-beltrán

paragraphs: Steven loaded a screen filled with dense lines of code.

paragraphs.text: "[frowning]Okay, looks clean. Am I missing something?" Steven said, frowning.
paragraphs.speaker: steven-gail

paragraphs.text: "[guiding]Look closer. Read between the lines," Dom challenged.
paragraphs.speaker: dominic-beltrán

paragraphs: He reached over Steven's shoulder, his fat finger tracing a specific path through the code.

paragraphs.text: "[explaining]The Directorate's techos will scan this. They'll see a standard calibration sequence, a set of commands that tells the gyroscope how to spin, how to balance, how to keep our cycle in sync with theirs. All down, yeah? All heavy."
paragraphs.speaker: dominic-beltrán

paragraphs: [#tap]He tapped a block of commented code titled: *//REDUNDANT ACOUSTIC BUFFER - DEPRECATED.*

paragraphs.text: "[low]But they won't check this part. It's old code from the ship's first test run, a buffer meant to handle sonic vibrations from the core. It's out of use."
paragraphs.speaker: dominic-beltrán

paragraphs.text: "[realizing]And you've reactivated it," Steven stated with a slow dawn of understanding.
paragraphs.speaker: steven-gail

paragraphs.text: "[grinning]Reactivated and taught it a new trick," Dom said, his grin widening.
paragraphs.speaker: dominic-beltrán

paragraphs: [#tapping]He made a few adjustments on the console.

paragraphs.text: "[mischievous]The grav-cycler doesn't just make gravity. It can sing. A subsonic hum that no one consciously hears. I've embedded a new set of commands in this old buffer."
paragraphs.speaker: dominic-beltrán

paragraphs.text: "[pressing]Commands to what?" Steven pressed, seeking clarification.
paragraphs.speaker: steven-gail

paragraphs.text: "[demonstrating]To this," Dom answered, tapping his slate.
paragraphs.speaker: dominic-beltrán

paragraphs: He finished his drink in one gulp, [#tap]pressed the glass to the wall, and put his ear to it.

paragraphs.text: "[inviting]Yes, go ahead. Try it."
paragraphs.speaker: dominic-beltrán

paragraphs: Steven looked at him, clearly skeptical but curious enough to join in. He finished his drink and copied Dom, pressing the glass to the wall and his ear to the glass.
At first, he thought it was noise — an ocean in a seashell. Then he could decipher a whisper, repeating the same phrase over and over.

paragraphs.text: *…never trust an atom… never trust an atom…*

paragraphs: Steven jerked his head from the glass. Dominic grinned ear to ear.

paragraphs.text: "[proud]That's our private party line," Dom said. "[explaining]I'm using the grav-cycler's resonance to make the hull vibrate. A whisper in the walls."
paragraphs.speaker: dominic-beltrán

paragraphs: [#tap]He tapped his slate again.

paragraphs.text: "[inviting]Go on. Try it again."
paragraphs.speaker: dominic-beltrán

paragraphs: Steven hesitated, then pressed the glass to the wall again. The whisper was different this time, repeating a new phrase.

paragraphs.text: Steven arched one eyebrow at Dom. "[deadpan]Because they make up everything?!"
paragraphs.speaker: steven-gail

paragraphs.text: "[shrugging]A test phrase." Dom shrugged.
paragraphs.speaker: dominic-beltrán

paragraphs.text: Steven sighed. "[toasting]Trust," he said, as he grabbed the bottle and poured a refill. "[toasting]Trust a Nulgee three-sixty."
paragraphs.speaker: steven-gail

paragraphs.text: "[toasting]Three-sixty," the Nulgee toasted.
paragraphs.speaker: dominic-beltrán

sections.heading: CAPTAIN'S QUARTERS, FNS DÁINN
paragraphs: Entries nested inside entries, time-stamped out of order. Course corrections. Crew rotations. Tonnage reports. Then Ionie found a branch flagged: *RESTRICTED.*

She opened it. The archive unfolded into fragments. Mission outlines and ship registries she did not recognize. Dispatched a decade earlier into the Yreus system. An expedition. The Yreus Relay Station appeared again and again.
Ionie already knew the station predated their arrival on Mairee. What arrested her was the detail that followed its construction. It had remained manned.

The logs spoke sideways about the station's function. Contact. Persistent presence. Whatever the station was for, it required people to stay. It did not surprise her that others had passed through the Fold before the fleet. Someone had to build the thing. Someone had to scout. Reconnaissance had always come first. What surprised her was how many had never returned. And, even now, the station was not unmanned.

She scrolled. Timelines thinned. Some ended mid-report. Others resumed months later. Calibration cycles that did not correspond to any known sensor array. Habitation quotas adjusted for crew fatigue and exposure. Rotations measured in thresholds rather than shifts. In several entries, the relay was described as a *buffer.*

There were protocols for events the logs refused to name. Directives to delay transmissions in communication between both sides of the Fold. To change them even. She noticed how often the word *interface* appeared. Then she read reports detailing the repurposing of cryopods. Into what, she could not decipher.
She paused. The next entry did not need interpretation. It confirmed contact had occurred. Reports followed detailing interactions.
Ben had known all along. And he had not been alone. Vilde's name appeared again and again, threaded through the margins. Farley, Reed, Heston, Savannen, Corbin.

paragraphs.text: *They knew before we went in.*

paragraphs: Ionie sat there for a long moment, hands resting on the slate.

sections.heading:
31 CLICKS POST UNFOLDING 
ORBITAL COMMAND
paragraphs: The *Dáinn* slid into its docking position at the high-orbital anchor with Orbital Command. Ulre watched the procedure, feeling quietly satisfied. Commander Bellamy's calm voice guided the vessel smoothly into the station's [#clamps]clamps.
[#chime]Ulre turned from the viewport when the confirmation chimed.

paragraphs.text: "[brisk]Are they ready?" he asked the guardian posted by the door.
paragraphs.speaker: ulre-corbin

paragraphs.text: "[firm]Yes. The captain will be escorted to the reception room," the guardian replied.
paragraphs.speaker: guardian

paragraphs: [#doorhiss]Ulre entered the reception room, which was small and unwelcoming despite its purpose. Ben Westhoff was brought in soon after, his hands behind his back. He wore his old, worn flight jacket as a small act of defiance.

paragraphs.text: "[cold]Westhoff," Ulre said, dropping any formality as the door closed behind him. "[cold]Your ship is now under the jurisdiction of the Directorate. As such, a change of command is in effect. You are hereby relieved of your duties as captain of the *Dáinn*."
paragraphs.speaker: ulre-corbin

paragraphs: Ulre noticed Ben had already removed his insignia. Ben simply met Ulre's gaze, unblinking. There was no anger, no shock. Only a quiet, unnerving stillness. It took a few moments before Ulre broke the silence, realizing he would get no response from Ben.

paragraphs.text: "[measured]We are in a state of crisis, and despite your insubordination, every asset must be utilized to its maximum potential."
paragraphs.speaker: ulre-corbin

paragraphs: He paused.

paragraphs.text: "[offering]I am offering you a reassignment. You will serve as foreman for Plateau Mining Crew Gamma. You'll oversee the operations you delayed and report to Chief Heston on the surface."
paragraphs.speaker: ulre-corbin

paragraphs: Ben listened without a flicker of emotion. Ulre expected a protest, an outburst. He got none. At last, Ben nodded slowly.

paragraphs.text: "[even]Understood, Director," he said in an even voice. "[even]When do I start?"
paragraphs.speaker: benjamin-westhoff

paragraphs: Ben's calm acceptance unsettled Ulre more than any argument would have. On the surface, Ben seemed like someone who had weighed his options and accepted the result. However, that calm made Ulre realize Ben must be playing a different game, and a sense of unease broke through his satisfaction.
He shook it off, reminding himself that whatever Ben was planning wouldn't matter. The board was his. He had the ship, the cargo, and the man.

paragraphs.text: "[brisk]Shuttle leaves in two beats," Ulre said, heading for the door. "[dismissive]No time for goodbyes. Dismissed."
paragraphs.speaker: ulre-corbin

paragraphs: [#doorhiss]Westhoff left the room without a word, and that unwelcome touch of doubt returned, seeping into Ulre.
Returning to the viewport, Ulre watched as the first heavy cargo shuttles disembarked, his gaze inevitably drawn to the one unresolved variable. The *Brisinger*.

To Ulre, the ship was still a ghost, moving silently toward him. Ulre anticipated that the ship would simply glide into its designated slot. Because of this predictable trajectory, there was no need to risk another vessel or attempt a complex interception. All he had to do was wait for it to fall into his lap.
Even with such certainties, the waiting was unbearable. He could not allow a variable of this magnitude to remain unmanaged, so he keyed his console.

paragraphs.text: "[clipped]Centurion Jax, my office."
paragraphs.speaker: ulre-corbin

paragraphs: [#doorhiss]The door hissed open, and the Centurion entered.

paragraphs.text: "[deep]Di-rec-tor," Jax said, thudding each syllable.
paragraphs.speaker: centurion-jax

paragraphs.text: "[direct]The Bris," Ulre said, pointing to the icon on the screen. "[direct]It will reach us next click."
paragraphs.speaker: ulre-corbin

paragraphs: Ulre noticed the wide dilation of Jax's pupils but continued regardless.

paragraphs.text: "[strategic]They may expect a warm welcome," Ulre said, "[strategic]but we are prepared for anything. Securing the protein synthesizers, the seed banks, and the Med-Bay stores is our primary concern. Secure the ship. The cargo. The crew. See to it."
paragraphs.speaker: ulre-corbin

paragraphs: Jax offered a terse inclination of the head and turned to depart.

Ulre tracked his movement with a lingering gaze. There was a primal composure to the man's posture. A relief coiled in Ulre's chest; it was a profound mercy that a creature of such intoxicating physical gravity possessed no hunger for the throne. To have a man like that crave power as he craved the fray would be to invite a ruin that Ulre wasn't entirely sure he would wish to survive.
